title: Jojo Rabbit
logline: A fanatical 10-year-old Hitler Youth member discovers his mother is hiding a Jewish girl in their attic, forcing him to confront his Nazi beliefs through an unlikely friendship.
antagonistOrConflict: Nazi ideology and Jojo's internal struggle between indoctrination and humanity
tone: Satirical yet heartfelt, balancing absurdist humor with genuine emotional weight
themes: The corruption of innocence by ideology
The power of human connection to overcome hatred
Coming of age through moral awakening

actBreaks.description: Jojo discovers Elsa hiding in his sister's room, launching the central conflict between his Nazi beliefs and human reality.

validation.ruling: Scene #21 shows Jojo sitting petrified on his bed with imaginary Hitler after discovering Elsa, representing the irreversible moment where his world fundamentally changes and he must navigate this new reality of harboring a Jewish girl.

actBreaks.description: After the town square revelation about his mother, Jojo's worldview fundamentally shifts as he begins to see through Nazi propaganda.

validation.ruling: Scene #69 shows Jojo sitting quietly in the town square at dusk next to his mother's body, with Hitler appearing to comfort him, marking the devastating low point that forces him into the final act of the story.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script employs a deceptively simple three-act structure that mirrors Jojo's psychological journey from blind fanaticism to moral awakening. The first act establishes Jojo's Nazi indoctrination through darkly comic Hitler Youth scenes, building to the shocking discovery of Elsa. The second act becomes an extended dance of deception and gradual understanding, as Jojo's relationship with Elsa slowly erodes his programmed hatred. The structure cleverly uses Hitler as Jojo's imaginary friend to externalize his internal conflict, making the abstract concrete.

The pacing accelerates dramatically in the final act as external war pressures collapse Jojo's insular world. The script's genius lies in how it uses the linear progression to show that overcoming hatred isn't a single moment of revelation, but a gradual process of humanization through genuine relationship.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains deliberate pacing in the middle section (scenes 21-69) to allow Jojo and Elsa's relationship to develop organically, with intimate conversation scenes balanced by broader war context. The final act accelerates rapidly from scene 70 onward, compressing time as the war's end approaches and Jojo must make his final moral choice.

incitingIncident.description: Jojo explores his dead sister Inga's room, examines her belongings, then discovers a thin cut in the wallpaper that reveals a secret room behind the wall.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #21 is already identified as an act break and cannot serve dual purpose. Scene #20 is the true inciting incident where Jojo discovers a secret room behind the wallpaper in his dead sister's bedroom, shattering his ordinary world and establishing the main conflict that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: Elsa tells Jojo an elaborate fictional story about Jews living in caves and loving art, while he takes notes for his book and they share a moment of connection.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - ELSA’S HIDEOUT - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #53 shows Jojo shifting from reactive fear to proactive engagement as he sits with Elsa listening to her fabricated story about Jews, marking his transformation from antagonist to someone genuinely connecting with her as a person rather than an enemy.

climax.description: Jojo and Elsa step outside where she sees Allied soldiers and realizes Nathan is alive, dropping her bag as Jojo does a small celebration and 'Heroes' begins to play.

validation.ruling: Scene #83 is internal character work but not the climactic confrontation. Scene #85 is the true climax where Jojo and Elsa step outside together, she sees the Allied soldiers and realizes Nathan is alive, representing the peak emotional moment and resolution of Jojo's character arc as he chooses love over hate.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Ten-year-old Jojo Betzler dresses in his Hitler Youth uniform and draws a Hitler mustache on his face while looking in the mirror.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo walks confidently down the street giving Hitler salutes to neighbors and meets his best friend Yorki, who joins him.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - HITLERJUGEND CAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: At Hitler Youth camp, children sing loyalty oaths to Hitler while Captain Klenzendorf and Sub-Officer Finkel address the young recruits about their training weekend.

scenes.heading: INT. WOODS - GROUP CIRCLE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Children sit in a circle calling out anti-Semitic descriptions of Jews while Fraulein Rahm writes them on a blackboard and adds her own hateful propaganda.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jojo and Yorki lie in sleeping bags discussing battle fantasies with their DJ knives, with Jojo boasting about dying laughing while Yorki admits he'd be scared.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - TRAINING GROUND - MORNING
scenes.summary: Team leaders Hans and Christoph challenge the children about killing, then hand Jojo a rabbit and order him to wring its neck.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo sits alone crying after being called a coward, then his imaginary friend Adolf Hitler appears to comfort him and encourage him to prove himself.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of Jojo's imaginary Hitler friend, establishing his internal psychological coping mechanism

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - TRAINING GROUND - DAY
scenes.summary: During grenade training, Jojo grabs a grenade to prove his bravery but it explodes, injuring him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jojo's injury fundamentally changes his physical state and removes him from active Hitler Youth participation

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo drifts in and out of consciousness in the hospital, seeing glimpses of doctors, his bloodied thumb, and imaginary Hitler giving him a thumbs up.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - JOJO’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jojo wakes at home hearing creaking sounds outside his door and whispers fearfully into the darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Jojo stands before his mirror with facial scars and a limp arm, greeting his mother who reassures him about his recovery and appearance.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOJO’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo's mother helps him with his jacket as he expresses reluctance to go outside, worried about people staring at his injuries.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - STREET / RATION STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo limps down the street with his mother, trying to avoid people's gazes while she enters a ration store.

scenes.heading: I/E. REICHSJUGENDAMT OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo and his mother part ways outside the Hitler Youth office, where she playfully tricks him with the old 'something on your shirt' gag.

scenes.heading: INT. REICHSJUGENDAMT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo enters the Hitler Youth office screaming 'Heil Hitler' and meets Captain Klenzendorf, who assigns him to distribute conscription notices.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo puts up Hitler propaganda posters on the street, giving enthusiastic Nazi salutes to increasingly unenthusiastic passersby.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE - FLORIDSDORFER SPITZ - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo walks through the town square and sees five bodies hanging from a makeshift gallows with signs around their necks.

scenes.heading: I/E. GRUSCH GROCERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo delivers a conscription notice to Herr Grusch, who reveals his son is dead and he's being recruited despite being an old veteran.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM
scenes.summary: Jojo arrives home calling for his mother, but she doesn't answer and he hears a thud from upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo explores his dead sister Inga's room, examines her belongings, then discovers a thin cut in the wallpaper that reveals a secret room behind the wall.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of the secret room sets up the central conflict of the story

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - JOJO’S BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo sits terrified on his bed while imaginary Hitler appears, and they discuss the Jewish girl's powers and speed, with Hitler offering cigarettes and planning solutions.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - UPSTAIRS HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo creeps down the hallway wearing makeshift armor made of household items and carrying a kitchen knife.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo stands in Inga's room speaking to the wall, politely asking the Jewish girl to leave and requesting his knife back.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - JOJO’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Back in his bedroom, Jojo and imaginary Hitler panic about the girl having two knives and brainstorm increasingly absurd solutions including gasoline and a giant mousetrap.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Jojo sits alone in the living room with a look of utter fear and confusion on his face.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - EVENING
scenes.summary: Jojo stands motionless in the kitchen holding bread, staring into space.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOJO’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - EVENING
scenes.summary: Jojo waits on his front steps while a neighbor peers through curtains across the street.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - FRAU BETLZER’S ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Jojo sits in his mother's room listening to his watch tick until he hears a car arrive and runs to meet her.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jojo confronts his mother about being out late and hearing 'her,' but his mother deflects by fixing his shoelaces and tucking him into bed.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - JOJO’S ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Frau Betzler tucks Jojo into bed and they discuss missing his father and sister, with her teaching him how to wink.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - BEDROOM/KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jojo lies awake and follows candlelight to see his mother preparing food and tiptoeing upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - UPSTAIRS HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jojo watches his mother knock on the wall, remove a panel, and feed Elsa whose bony white arm extends from the darkness to take the food.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jojo witnesses his mother feeding Elsa, confirming the conspiracy and his mother's involvement

scenes.heading: INT. REHABILITATION CENTRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo swims in a rehabilitation pool with wounded soldiers while imaginary Hitler swims nearby wearing a bathing cap and making disgusted faces at the disabled men.

scenes.heading: INT. REICHSJUGENDAMT - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Hitler Youth office, Jojo finds Klenzendorf drawing fantasy weapons while Finkel plays with pink triangle patches, and Klenzendorf mentions helping the Gestapo with investigations.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Jojo and Elsa establish a stalemate agreement where he won't turn her in if she tells him everything about Jews, with her holding his knife and sitting in her hideout doorway.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct negotiation between Jojo and Elsa establishes their ongoing relationship dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Jojo waits in the living room while imaginary Hitler appears dressed as a Native American, then panics and hides when Frau Betzler arrives home.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jojo and his mother eat dinner while she cheerfully discusses the Allies taking Italy and the war ending soon.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: During dinner cleanup, Jojo watches his mother scrape leftovers onto a plate and suggests getting rid of Inga's belongings.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jojo interrogates Elsa about Jewish life while bombs drop in the distance, with her giving sarcastic answers about her family and mocking his lack of friends.

scenes.heading: INT. GESTAPO OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo visits the Gestapo office to search for information about someone, with a secretary directing him to the chaotic file room.

scenes.heading: INT. GESTAPO OFFICE - FILE ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo searches through messy files looking for 'Nathan Raame' while Erwin scratches himself, but finds nothing.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN - LIBRARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo steals a Rilke poetry book from the library while imaginary Hitler stuffs books into his jacket for an elaborate trap involving piranhas.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - LIBRARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Leaving the library, Jojo encounters a group of Hitler Youth marching and singing, who look at him with contempt.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jojo writes a letter on his bed with the stolen Rilke book nearby while imaginary Hitler reads about camels.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - JOJO’S ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo and Hitler sit in darkness listening to footsteps, then Hitler signals for action as Jojo moves into the hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Jojo reads Elsa a fake letter from Nathan breaking up with her, written in childish handwriting, causing her visible distress.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo returns to read Elsa a second fake letter from Nathan taking back the breakup to prevent her suicide.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo and Elsa engage in a playful debate about famous Germans versus Jews, laughing together as they trade names like Beethoven, Einstein, and Freud.
scenes.turningPointNote: First moment of genuine connection and humanity between Jojo and Elsa, showing his growing empathy

scenes.heading: EXT. FOOD RATION STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Frau Betzler uses three ration cards at the food store, giving the storeman a confusing mathematical explanation for why she needs more food.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - RIVER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Jojo and his mother sit by the river watching a pathetic parade of returned soldiers while she speaks about romance and love being the strongest thing in the world.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - STREET - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo and his mother walk through town as other children mock him behind his back, and she scares them away before asking him to promise to be a normal kid when the war ends.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOJO’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo and his mother scrub graffiti reading 'O-5' off their front door while she dismisses it as a prank and neighbors watch.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - ELSA’S HIDEOUT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elsa tells Jojo an elaborate fictional story about Jews living in caves and loving art, while he takes notes for his book and they share a moment of connection.
scenes.turningPointNote: First genuine bonding moment between Jojo and Elsa, marking shift from antagonism to friendship

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - JOJO’S BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Adolf Hitler confronts Jojo in his bedroom about getting too close to Elsa, expressing discomfort about their growing friendship.

scenes.heading: INT. REICHSJUGENDAMT - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo visits Captain Klenzendorf at the Nazi office asking about rewards for turning in Jews while sharing absurd 'facts' about Jewish behavior.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo attempts to collect metal for the war effort from an old woman who doesn't understand him and gives him nothing.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - FLORIDSDORFER SPITZ - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo encounters his friend Yorki, now dressed as a child soldier in a cardboard uniform, while collecting scrap metal around town.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo gives Elsa pencils and paper as a gift, then asks her to draw him again while expressing insecurity about his scarred face.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Elsa bathes in the bathroom while Jojo sits outside listening to the sounds of her washing.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - FRAU BETZLER’S ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo watches Elsa put on makeup and do her hair in his mother's room, feeling strange sensations in his stomach.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo brings Elsa downstairs to share a meal of barely cooked potatoes when loud knocking at the door forces her to hide.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Four Gestapo agents led by Captain Deertz arrive at the house with Klenzendorf to search for suspicious activity, exchanging multiple 'Heil Hitler' greetings.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - UPSTAIRS HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Elsa emerges disguised as Jojo's dead sister Inga, boldly confronting the Gestapo agents and successfully fooling them with her performance.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elsa's life-or-death deception of the Gestapo, the highest stakes moment so far

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Gestapo agents leave after being fooled, and Klenzendorf dismisses Jojo from his duties while giving him a knowing smile.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - ELSA’S HIDEOUT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Elsa realizes she gave the wrong birthdate for Inga and panics that the Gestapo will return and discover the deception, wanting to flee.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Adolf Hitler stirs hot water in the kitchen and questions Jojo about why the Gestapo believed Elsa's disguise.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - FOOD STORE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Jojo receives meager rations of bread and butter at the food store in exchange for his ration card.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE - FLORIDSDORFER SPITZ - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Jojo follows a butterfly through the town square and discovers something that casts moving shadows above him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE - FLORIDSDORFER SPITZ - DUSK
scenes.summary: Jojo sits quietly in the park at dusk when Adolf Hitler appears beside him with sympathetic eyes, touches his shoulder, and disappears.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jojo's discovery of his mother's execution, though not explicitly shown, fundamentally changes everything

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - ELSA’S HIDEOUT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jojo enters Elsa's room with a knife and attempts to attack her multiple times, but she stops him and he collapses, crying for his mama.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOJO’S HOUSE - ROOFTOP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the rooftop, Elsa explains to Jojo that his mother was part of the Austrian resistance and his father was involved from afar, not fighting for the Nazis.
scenes.turningPointNote: Revelation that Jojo's parents were anti-Nazi resistance fighters, shattering his worldview

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: In spring, Jojo reads Elsa a fake letter praising her artwork from a fictional Jewish gallery owner in New York.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - JOJO’S ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo stores Elsa's drawings in his drawer with many others as distant explosions and gunshots shake the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRUSCH GROCERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo encounters civilians being armed for battle and sees Yorki accidentally fire a rocket launcher, destroying a storefront.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE - BEETHOVEN PLATZ - DAY
scenes.summary: Yorki tells Jojo the Russians are approaching and warns they eat babies and have sex with dogs, while Jojo confesses Elsa is his Jewish girlfriend.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Allied soldiers parade through the streets as a woman tells Jojo the war is over and they will have peace.
scenes.turningPointNote: The war ends, fundamentally changing the world Jojo knows

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - STREET - LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo stands in line for Russian rations and discovers a makeshift morgue of dead German soldiers, taking a coat from one of the bodies.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Jojo tells Elsa the war is over and she celebrates, wanting to go outside, but he lies and says Nathan is dead to keep her from leaving.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jojo's desperate lie to prevent Elsa from leaving him reveals his transformation and attachment

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL - CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A Russian teacher instructs German children that Nazis are monsters while writing negative words about Germans on the blackboard.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE - FLORIDSDORFER SPITZ - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo witnesses Russian soldiers looting and sees former Nazi officials including Klenzendorf being rounded up and beaten by angry mobs.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN - RIVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Yorki tells Jojo that Hitler killed himself in a bunker, which Jojo refuses to believe.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - INGA’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo reads Elsa another fake letter from Nathan, this time saying he's proud of Jojo for taking care of her, but gets no response.

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - JOJO’S BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jojo confronts Adolf Hitler in his bedroom, rejecting their friendship and calling him a coward for lying and killing himself, then kicks him out the window.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jojo finally rejects Nazi ideology by literally kicking Hitler out of his life

scenes.heading: INT. JOJO’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jojo leads Elsa through the house toward the front door, noticing her drawings on his wall, and ties her shoelaces before they exit.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOJO’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jojo and Elsa step outside where she sees Allied soldiers and realizes Nathan is alive, dropping her bag as Jojo does a small celebration and 'Heroes' begins to play.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elsa discovers Jojo's lie and realizes she's truly free, completing both characters' journeys

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
